title: Fixing Broken HTML List Items with Invalid 'type' Attributes

statement.markdown: Implement a function that takes an HTML string as input and returns a boolean indicating whether all 'li' tags have a valid 'type' attribute. A 'type' attribute is considered valid if it matches one of the following values: 'disc', 'circle', 'square', 'none', or 'decimal'. The function should ignore any 'li' tags without a 'type' attribute.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether all 'li' tags have valid 'type' attributes
statement.notes: The function should not modify the input HTML string. It should only check the validity of 'type' attributes in 'li' tags.

constraints.description: The number of 'li' tags in the HTML string

examples.explanation: The 'type' attribute in the third 'li' tag is invalid.
All 'type' attributes in the 'li' tags are valid.

hints.text: Use a DOM parser to parse the HTML string and traverse the DOM tree.
Check the value of each 'type' attribute in the 'li' tags and verify if it matches one of the valid values.

editorial.title: Fixing Broken HTML List Items with Invalid 'type' Attributes
editorial.intuition: To fix this problem, we need to parse the HTML string and check the value of each 'type' attribute in the 'li' tags. If the value is not one of the valid values, we return false.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use a simple string search to check if the 'type' attribute value is one of the valid values.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a DOM parser to parse the HTML string and traverse the DOM tree. This will allow us to efficiently check the value of each 'type' attribute in the 'li' tags.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a DOM parser to parse the HTML string and traverse the DOM tree. Then, we can use a loop to check the value of each 'type' attribute in the 'li' tags. If any of the values are not one of the valid values, we return false. Otherwise, we return true.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function correctly checks the value of each 'type' attribute in the 'li' tags and returns true if all values are valid and false otherwise.
editorial.dryRun:
Suppose we have the following HTML string:
  • Item 1
  • Item 2
  • Item 3
  • Item 4
. The function will parse the HTML string, traverse the DOM tree, and check the value of each 'type' attribute in the 'li' tags. It will return false because the 'type' attribute value in the third 'li' tag is invalid.
commonMistakes: Not checking the value of the 'type' attribute
Not considering cases where the 'type' attribute is missing
